QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_39

question.text: As per SEBI regulations, the position limit for FPI Category I, Mutual Funds, Trading Members (proprietary), and clients in index futures contracts is:

answer.text: Higher of 15% of total open interest in index futures or Rs. 500 crore
Higher of 10% of total open interest or Rs. 500 crore
Fixed at Rs. 1,500 crore for all participants

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For index futures, the position limit for FPI Category I, Mutual Funds, TMs (proprietary), and clients is the higher of 15% of total OI in index futures or Rs. 500 crore. FPI Category II has different limits (10% or 5% of OI depending on sub-category). For index options, separate PAN-level limits apply: net EOD FutEq OI of Rs. 1,500 crore and gross FutEq OI of Rs. 10,000 crore.
**The Trap:** Rs. 1,500 crore is the PAN-level limit for index options (net EOD), not for index futures. Don't confuse the two. Also, FPI Cat II has lower limits than Cat I.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Position limits vary by instrument type (index futures vs index options vs stock derivatives) and participant category.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Index futures client limit: higher of 15% OI or Rs. 500 Cr. Index options PAN limit: Rs. 1,500 Cr net / Rs. 10,000 Cr gross. Stock derivatives client limit: higher of 1% free float or 5% of OI.
